title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator – Printer Not Printing / Paper Jam (Thermal Paper)
description: Structured Clinical Engineering approach for resolving printer not printing or paper jam errors on a ZOLL R Series defibrillator.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Do not troubleshoot on an active code or patient.
If the device is in clinical use, move the patient to another defibrillator before proceeding.
Patient care always takes priority over equipment troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check the Paper Stack
steps.instructions:
Open the printer compartment.
Confirm a paper stack is present.
Ensure the stack is properly seated and aligned in the tray.
Verify the top sheet’s leading edge is correctly positioned in the feed path.
Replace the stack if:
It is empty
Edges are curled or bent
Sheets are wrinkled or compressed
Paper appears old or heat-damaged
Thermal paper that is creased or compressed can misfeed and trigger jam errors.

steps.title: Inspect for Paper Jam or Debris
steps.instructions:
With the cover open:
Remove any misfed or partially fed sheets.
Look for torn paper fragments inside the feed rollers.
Check for debris or adhesive residue.
Ensure rollers appear clean and unobstructed.
Do not use sharp tools that could damage the thermal print head.
Clear the path completely before closing the cover.

steps.title: Verify Printer Cover Closure
steps.instructions:
The printer will not operate if the cover is not fully latched.
Close the printer cover firmly.
Listen and feel for the latch click.
Confirm the cover sits flush and does not spring open.
A partially latched cover can generate false jam or “Insert Paper” messages.

steps.title: Try a Known-Good Thermal Paper Stack
steps.instructions:
If available:
Replace the current paper stack with a new, known-good thermal stack.
Ensure it is the correct type for the R Series.
Perform a test strip or print an event summary.
This step helps rule out paper quality issues.

steps.title: Observe and Record the Exact Display Message
steps.instructions:
Document the exact error shown:
“Paper Jam”
“Insert Paper”
“Printer Error”
No error, but no print output
Precise wording is important for trending and repair diagnostics.

CCR.Complaint: “Printer will not print / displaying paper jam error.”
CCR.Cause:
“Paper stack wrinkled and misaligned in tray.”
“Small torn paper fragment lodged in feed path.”
“Paper loaded correctly, cover secured, known-good paper used — printer still displays jam error. Suspected internal printer mechanism failure.”
CCR.Resolution:
“Cleared jam and replaced thermal paper stack; test print successful.”
“Re-seated paper and secured cover; printer operational.”
“Unit removed from service and sent for repair due to persistent printer error.”

helpfulDetails: Paper stack loaded correctly: yes / no
Printer cover fully latched: yes / no
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator – Pads Not Detected / Therapy Cable Failure
description: Structured troubleshooting guide for pad detection and therapy cable errors on a ZOLL R Series defibrillator.

steps.title: Verify Pads Are Properly Connected
steps.instructions: Ensure the therapy pads are fully seated into the therapy cable connector. Confirm the therapy cable is securely locked into the defibrillator front port. Disconnect and firmly reseat both connections.

steps.title: Inspect Pads for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Examine the pads for torn or cracked electrode surfaces, damaged snap connectors, dried or lifted gel surface, and contamination on contact surfaces. Replace immediately if any defect is noted.

steps.title: Inspect the Therapy Cable
steps.instructions: Visually and physically inspect the entire cable for fraying or exposed wiring, kinks or sharp bends near strain relief, cracked housing at the connector, and loose or bent pins. Gently flex the cable near both ends while observing the screen for intermittent recognition.

steps.title: Verify Pad Expiration Date
steps.instructions: Confirm pads are within manufacturer expiration date. If expired, replace with in-date pads and retest.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Cable or Pad Set
steps.instructions: Replace pads with a known-good set. If the issue persists, replace the therapy cable with a known-good cable. This step isolates whether the problem follows the pads, the cable, or remains with the defibrillator.

steps.title: Observe the Exact Display Message
steps.instructions: Document the exact wording of the error, whether the message appears immediately on power-up, whether it is intermittent, and any audible tones or status indicators.

steps.title: If the Problem Persists
steps.instructions: Remove the device from clinical service, label the unit Out of Service, and send for bench evaluation or authorized repair.

steps.title: Clinical Use Tip
steps.instructions: Immediately transfer the patient to a backup defibrillator and ensure therapy capability is restored before beginning equipment evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: “R Series defibrillator displays ‘Pads Not Detected’ during daily check.”
CCR.Cause: “Therapy cable intermittently loses connection when flexed near strain relief.” or “Pads expired and not recognized by device.” or “No issue found with pads or cable; suspected internal therapy board fault.”
CCR.Resolution: “Replaced expired pads; unit passed operational check and returned to service.” “Removed defective therapy cable from service and replaced with known-good cable.” “Unit removed from service and sent to vendor for evaluation.”

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator – Battery Issue Fails Self-Test
description: Structured Clinical Engineering troubleshooting guide for ZOLL R Series battery self-test failures.

steps.title: Ensure the Device Is Not in Active Clinical Use
steps.instructions: Before troubleshooting, confirm the defibrillator is not connected to a patient. If the unit is needed for care: Replace it immediately with a known-good device. Remove the affected unit from the crash cart. Self-test and battery troubleshooting should never be performed on an active emergency device.

steps.title: Verify the Battery Is Fully Seated
steps.instructions: Remove the battery from the rear compartment. Inspect the latch mechanism and guide rails. Reinstall the battery firmly until it clicks and locks into place. Why this matters: A partially seated battery can interrupt power continuity and trigger a self-test failure even if the battery itself is functional. Power the unit on and allow the self-test to complete.

steps.title: Inspect Battery and Device Contacts
steps.instructions: Visually inspect: Battery terminals Device-side contact pins Signs of debris, corrosion, discoloration, or bent contacts If debris is present, clean using an approved method per department policy. Why this matters: Poor electrical contact can cause charging faults, communication errors, or false self-test failure messages. Reinstall the battery and repeat the self-test.

steps.title: Verify Charging Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the defibrillator is stored connected to AC power per department policy. Check for: Charging indicator lights Docking station power (if applicable) Verified AC outlet functionality If needed, allow the battery to charge for an appropriate period and then repeat the self-test. Why this matters: Low capacity or deep discharge conditions can trigger battery failure messages even when the battery is not permanently defective.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Battery (If Available)
steps.instructions: Remove the suspect battery. Install a known-good battery from another ZOLL R Series unit. Allow the self-test to complete. Interpretation: If the alternate battery passes → Likely failed battery pack If the alternate battery also fails → Possible device-side power management issue This step isolates whether the problem follows the battery or remains with the device.

steps.title: Document the Exact Self-Test Message
steps.instructions: Record the exact wording displayed: “Battery Fail” “Replace Battery” Immediate failure vs failure after charge cycle Small differences in wording may indicate: Capacity failure Communication error Charging fault Precise documentation improves repair accuracy and vendor communication if escalation is required.

CCR.Complaint: Unit reports battery failed self-test during daily check.
CCR.Cause: Battery fails self-test after reseating and charging. Known-good battery passes. Suspected failed battery pack. OR Multiple batteries fail in same unit. Suspected internal device power issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed battery from service and replaced with known-good battery. Tagged battery Out of Service and sent for evaluation. Removed device from service pending bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual heat, swelling, or odor
Final device status (returned to service or removed)

title: ZOLL X Series Defibrillator - Battery Won’t Charge or Hold Power
description: Troubleshooting guide for situations where a ZOLL X Series defibrillator battery will not charge or fails to hold power.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not connected to a patient before troubleshooting. Move the patient to another defibrillator if necessary. Defibrillators must remain immediately available for emergency use.

steps.title: Check the Battery Installation
steps.instructions: Remove the battery and reinstall it securely. Ensure the battery is fully seated and latched. A partially seated battery may prevent proper charging or power transfer.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts
steps.instructions: Examine the battery contacts and the device contacts. Look for dirt, debris, corrosion, or bent contacts. Clean gently with approved cleaning wipes if contamination is present.

steps.title: Verify Charger or Dock Power
steps.instructions: If the device is placed in a charging dock or connected to AC power, confirm the dock is plugged into a known-good outlet. Verify the outlet provides power by testing with another device. Ensure any power indicator lights on the dock are illuminated.

steps.title: Check the Battery Charging Indicator
steps.instructions: Observe the charging indicators on the battery or device. No indicator may suggest poor contact or charger failure. Flashing or error lights may indicate battery end-of-life or fault.

steps.title: Swap With a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: Install a battery that is known to be functioning properly. If the device powers normally with the replacement battery, the original battery is likely defective. If the problem persists, the issue may be with the device or charger.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Battery in Another Device or Charger
steps.instructions: Place the battery into another compatible X Series charger or defibrillator. If it fails to charge there as well, the battery has likely reached end-of-life.

steps.title: Check Battery Age and Cycle Count (If Available)
steps.instructions: Batteries degrade over time and after repeated charge cycles. If the battery is older or heavily used, replacement may be appropriate.

steps.title: Observe for Abnormal Conditions
steps.instructions: Check for signs such as battery swelling, excess heat, or unusual odor. These indicate battery failure and the battery should be removed from service immediately.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing staff reported the defibrillator battery would not charge and the device shut down after being removed from the charger.
CCR.Cause: Battery failed to charge in multiple X Series chargers and would not power the device, indicating battery end-of-life.
CCR.Resolution: Battery removed from service and replaced with a new battery. Device tested and confirmed to charge and operate normally.

helpfulDetails: Battery swapped with known good battery
Any signs of swelling, heat, or damage

title: ZOLL X Series Defibrillator - Pads / Electrode Detection Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting ZOLL X Series defibrillator pads and electrode detection errors, emphasizing external checks and patient safety.

steps.title: Verify the Device Is Not in Active Clinical Use
steps.instructions: Ensure the defibrillator is not connected to a patient before troubleshooting. Move the patient to another device if necessary.

steps.title: Check the Pad Connector at the Defibrillator
steps.instructions: Confirm the therapy electrode cable is fully seated in the therapy port on the X Series. Remove and reconnect the cable. Ensure the connector is properly aligned and locked. Loose or partially seated connectors are a common cause of pad detection errors.

steps.title: Inspect the Pads (Electrodes)
steps.instructions: Check the therapy pads for expiration date, damaged adhesive or gel, broken lead wires, and packaging damage. Expired or damaged pads may not be detected correctly by the device.

steps.title: Verify the Correct Pad Type
steps.instructions: Ensure the pads being used are compatible with the ZOLL X Series (such as ZOLL OneStep electrodes or approved equivalents). Using incompatible pads may cause detection or impedance errors.

steps.title: Check the Therapy Cable
steps.instructions: Inspect the therapy electrode cable for bent pins, cracked insulation, kinks or stress near the connector, and loose strain relief. Damage to the cable can interrupt electrode detection.

steps.title: Swap with Known Good Pads
steps.instructions: Connect a new set of pads from sealed packaging. If the error clears, the original pads were likely defective or expired.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Therapy Cable (If Available)
steps.instructions: If another compatible cable is available: Disconnect the existing therapy cable, connect the known good cable, attach pads and power on the device. If the error resolves, the original cable is likely faulty.

steps.title: Check for Debris in the Connector Port
steps.instructions: Look inside the therapy cable port on the defibrillator. Ensure there is no debris, bent contacts, or contamination preventing proper electrical contact.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the device off, wait several seconds, and power it back on. Temporary detection errors can sometimes clear with a restart.

steps.title: Perform a Functional Test
steps.instructions: After reconnecting pads: Verify the device recognizes the pads, check that impedance values appear normal, confirm no electrode warnings remain.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the ZOLL X Series defibrillator displaying “Pads Not Detected” despite new electrodes being connected.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found the therapy electrode cable had visible strain damage near the connector, causing intermittent electrode detection.
CCR.Resolution: Therapy cable replaced and device tested with new pads. Pads detected normally and device passed functional check.

title: ZOLL X Series Defibrillator – ECG Display Errors or Artifacts
description: Guide for troubleshooting ECG display errors or artifacts on ZOLL X Series defibrillators, focusing on external checks and proper setup before internal diagnostics.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to an alternative monitored device if available. Do not troubleshoot on an actively deteriorating patient.

steps.title: Check ECG Electrode Placement and Skin Contact
steps.instructions: Verify electrodes are correctly placed and oriented according to standard 3- or 5-lead configuration. Ensure skin is clean, dry, and free of lotions or oils. Replace electrodes if they are old, dried out, or visibly defective.

steps.title: Inspect ECG Lead Wires and Connectors
steps.instructions: Look for frayed wires, broken insulation, bent or corroded connectors. Confirm connectors are fully seated in the defibrillator ports. Swap with a known-good lead set to determine if the issue persists.

steps.title: Check Device and Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Ensure the ECG leads are not touching metal surfaces or IV lines, which can introduce interference. Avoid running long lead wires alongside other electrical equipment that could cause noise.

steps.title: Verify Device Settings and Filters
steps.instructions: Confirm ECG gain, filter settings, and lead selection match intended monitoring setup. Reset to default settings if configuration is unclear or corrupted.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Defibrillator
steps.instructions: Turn off the device, wait 10–20 seconds, then power it back on. Observe if the ECG signal stabilizes after reboot.

CCR.Complaint: ECG waveform is erratic and shows intermittent artifacts.
CCR.Cause: Electrode contact poor and leads suspected of damage; artifact persists despite proper placement.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced electrodes and ECG lead set; device rebooted. If issue continues, removed device from service for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any observed interference (IV pumps, other electrical devices)
Device final status (e.g., returned to service or labeled out of service)

title: ZOLL X Series Defibrillator - Shock Delivery Issues / Charging Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting shock delivery failures or charging errors on ZOLL X Series defibrillators.

steps.title: Verify Power Source and Battery
steps.instructions: Ensure the device is plugged into a known-good AC outlet or wall charger. Check battery charge level; replace with a charged battery if below operational threshold. Reason: Defibrillators may not charge or deliver shocks if battery is low or faulty.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts and Connection
steps.instructions: Remove battery and inspect terminals for dirt, corrosion, or damage. Reinsert battery securely until it clicks. Reason: Poor contact can prevent charging and shock delivery.

steps.title: Check Pads and Cables
steps.instructions: Confirm electrodes/pads are connected correctly. Inspect for visible damage or wear; replace if necessary. Reason: The device may inhibit shock delivery if pad or cable errors are detected.

steps.title: Perform Self-Test / Daily Test
steps.instructions: Initiate device self-test or daily test as per device prompts. Observe for any error codes or warnings during the test. Reason: The device’s internal diagnostics can identify specific failures preventing shock delivery.

steps.title: Inspect Device for External Damage or Error Indicators
steps.instructions: Look for warning lights, error messages, or unusual beeping patterns. Ensure the device hasn’t been exposed to moisture, extreme temperatures, or physical shock. Reason: Environmental or physical damage can interfere with charging circuits and shock capacitors.

CCR.Complaint: Device will not deliver shocks; charging errors appear on display.
CCR.Cause: Observed battery was low, and self-test reported charging error despite good outlet. Pads and cables intact.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair to ZOLL-authorized service center.

helpfulDetails: Battery charge level and last replacement date
Pad/cable condition and last replacement date
Any unusual sounds, lights, or smells during operation

title: ZOLL X Series Defibrillator - Printer or Data Recording Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting printer or data recording failures on a ZOLL X Series defibrillator, focusing on external checks before assuming internal hardware issues.

steps.title: Check the Paper and Consumables
steps.instructions: Ensure the printer has paper loaded correctly and the roll is not empty. Verify the paper type matches manufacturer specifications. Reason: A missing or incompatible paper roll is the most common cause of print failures.

steps.title: Inspect Printer Mechanism
steps.instructions: Open the printer cover and check for jams, misaligned paper, or foreign objects. Clean rollers and sensors if visible debris is present. Reason: Paper jams or dust can prevent the printer from feeding correctly.

steps.title: Verify Printer Settings
steps.instructions: Access the defibrillator menu and confirm printing is enabled for events, ECG, or reports. Check for date/time settings, which can affect event logging. Reason: Incorrect settings may prevent data from being recorded or printed.

steps.title: Check Connections
steps.instructions: For external printers or data interfaces, ensure cables are fully seated and undamaged. Confirm any USB or network connections to recording devices are functional. Reason: Loose or damaged connections can interrupt data transfer.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn off the defibrillator, wait 30 seconds, then power it back on. Reason: A soft reset can resolve temporary firmware or memory glitches affecting printing.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Consumable or External Device
steps.instructions: Swap the paper roll or try printing to another compatible device if available. Reason: Confirms whether the issue is with the printer hardware or consumable.

CCR.Complaint: Printer not printing or event logs not recording.
CCR.Cause: Printer paper was empty and the feed mechanism had minor obstruction.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced paper roll, cleared feed path, verified print settings, and test print completed successfully.

helpfulDetails: Paper type and roll size verified
Printer cover and feed path inspected

title: ZOLL AED Plus Defibrillator - Battery Not Recognized or Replace Battery Alert
description: Troubleshooting battery recognition issues on the ZOLL AED Plus, focusing on correct 123A battery type, installation, and contact integrity.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If needed, replace with a backup unit before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the AED off and back on. This resets basic system checks and may clear a temporary battery recognition fault.

steps.title: Verify Correct Battery Type
steps.instructions: Ensure all installed batteries are 123A lithium batteries approved for the AED Plus. Do not use AA batteries or other chemistries.

steps.title: Check Battery Installation Orientation
steps.instructions: Remove and reinstall all batteries, ensuring correct polarity. Even one incorrectly installed battery can cause the alert.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Compartment Contacts
steps.instructions: Visually inspect for corrosion, debris, or bent contacts. Clean minor corrosion with a dry cloth if present. Poor contact prevents proper voltage detection.

steps.title: Replace All Batteries as a Set
steps.instructions: Install a full new set of fresh 123A lithium batteries. Do not mix old and new batteries. The AED evaluates the entire battery pack; uneven voltage can trigger alerts.

steps.title: Check Battery Date Codes
steps.instructions: Ensure batteries are not expired or near expiration. Older batteries may not provide stable voltage even if unused.

steps.title: Confirm Battery Compartment is Fully Seated
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery cover is properly closed and latched. Improper closure can interrupt battery contact.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Status
steps.instructions: After reinstalling batteries, check the Rescue Ready indicator. Green check indicates the issue is resolved. Red X or audible alert indicates the problem persists.

steps.title: Run Self-Test (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Allow the AED to complete its automatic self-test or initiate one if available. This confirms whether the battery is accepted by the system.

CCR.Complaint: AED Plus displaying 'Replace Battery' alert despite recent 123A battery replacement.
CCR.Cause: Incorrect battery type or corroded battery contacts preventing proper voltage detection.
CCR.Resolution: Installed new set of 123A lithium batteries, cleaned contacts, and verified green readiness indicator.

helpfulDetails: Batteries replaced as a full set of 123A lithium
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: ZOLL AED Plus Defibrillator - Pad Connection Errors or Pad Not Detected
description: Addresses pad connection errors or pads not detected, focusing on pad placement, cable integrity, and external connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active use. If needed, replace with a backup unit before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Pads Are Installed Correctly
steps.instructions: Open the AED cover and confirm pads are properly connected to the internal pad connector. Loose or partially inserted connectors commonly trigger detection errors. Reseat the connector firmly and ensure it clicks into place.

steps.title: Check Pad Expiration Date
steps.instructions: Inspect the electrode pad expiration date. Expired pads may fail self-test or not be recognized. Replace with a new, in-date pad set if expired.

steps.title: Inspect Pad Packaging and Integrity
steps.instructions: Ensure pads are sealed and not dried out or damaged. Torn packaging or dried gel can cause poor conductivity and detection issues. Replace pads if any damage is noted.

steps.title: Inspect Pad Cable and Connector
steps.instructions: Visually check the cable from pads to AED for cuts, fraying, or kinks and bent or damaged connector pins. If damage is found, replace the pad set.

steps.title: Reconnect and Power Cycle the AED
steps.instructions: Disconnect the pads, wait 10 seconds, then reconnect and power cycle the AED. This allows the unit to reinitialize and detect the pads. If error clears, return device to service.

steps.title: Check Internal Pad Storage Area
steps.instructions: Ensure pads are properly seated in the lid and routed correctly. Improper storage can stress the cable or prevent proper connection. Reposition pads and cable neatly.

steps.title: Run Self-Test / Observe Indicator
steps.instructions: Close the lid and verify the Rescue Ready indicator shows green. If indicator remains red or beeping persists, issue is unresolved.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Pads
steps.instructions: Install a new, known good pad set. If the issue resolves, original pads were faulty. If issue persists, likely a device-side connector or sensing issue.

CCR.Complaint: AED Plus reporting pad connection error and not detecting electrodes during readiness check.
CCR.Cause: Faulty electrode pad set with degraded cable connection.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced pads with new set; verified proper connection and normal Rescue Ready indicator.

helpfulDetails: Pads replaced with known good set

title: ZOLL AED Plus Defibrillator - Self-Test Failure on Power-On
description: Addresses self-test failures during power-on, focusing on battery status, pad connections, and external readiness conditions affecting device diagnostics.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active use. If needed, replace with a backup unit before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Status and Audible Alerts
steps.instructions: Check the Rescue Ready indicator and listen for beeps or voice prompts. Note any error messages or tones, as these help identify the failure source.

steps.title: Verify Battery Installation and Type
steps.instructions: Open the battery compartment and confirm all batteries are installed correctly with proper polarity. Ensure approved batteries (ZOLL-recommended type) are being used. Avoid mixing old and new batteries. Improper battery setup is a common cause of self-test failure.

steps.title: Replace All Batteries with New Set
steps.instructions: Install a full, fresh set of new batteries. Even if some appear good, partial depletion can fail the self-test. Power cycle the device after replacement. If the issue resolves, remove from troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts
steps.instructions: Check battery terminals for corrosion, debris, or bent/recessed contacts. Clean lightly if needed using appropriate method; poor contact can interrupt self-test.

steps.title: Check Electrode (Pad) Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure pads are properly plugged into the device, seated firmly, and within expiration date. The AED performs readiness checks that include pad presence and integrity.

steps.title: Inspect Pad Cable and Connector
steps.instructions: Look for frayed or damaged cables, loose or worn connectors, or broken locking tabs. Swap with a known-good pad set if available.

steps.title: Confirm Pads Are Properly Stored
steps.instructions: Verify pads are placed correctly in the lid and not partially removed. Improper storage can trigger readiness or self-test errors.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off and back on after completing checks. Observe whether the self-test completes successfully.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure the unit is not exposed to extreme temperatures or moisture/condensation. These can affect internal diagnostics during startup.

CCR.Complaint: AED failed self-test on power-on and did not display ready status.
CCR.Cause: Depleted batteries and poor pad connection prevented successful self-test completion.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced all batteries and installed new electrode pads; device passed self-test and returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Audible beep pattern or voice prompts
Environmental conditions at time of failure

title: ZOLL AED Plus Defibrillator - Rescue Ready Indicator Not Showing Green
description: Addresses the “Rescue Ready” indicator failing to show green, focusing on battery, pads, device self-test, and external readiness conditions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If needed, replace with a backup AED before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Battery Status
steps.instructions: Verify the battery is properly installed. Confirm the battery is charged and recognized by the device. Expected outcome: Device recognizes battery, no alerts; indicator may turn green.

steps.title: Inspect Pads
steps.instructions: Ensure pads are correctly installed and connected to the AED. Check pad expiration dates and integrity. Expected outcome: Pads recognized; no pad error messages.

steps.title: Observe Device Self-Test Results
steps.instructions: Power on the AED and allow it to complete its automatic self-test. Note any error messages displayed. Expected outcome: Self-test completes successfully; “Rescue Ready” indicator should turn green.

steps.title: Verify Device Placement and Environment
steps.instructions: Ensure the AED is on a stable surface, not in extreme temperatures, and free from moisture. Why this matters: Environmental factors can prevent successful self-test completion.

steps.title: Check for Accessories or Firmware Updates
steps.instructions: If your department tracks firmware versions, confirm the device has the latest approved version. Remove any non-standard accessories that might interfere with readiness checks. Expected outcome: Device free of external interference; indicator turns green.

CCR.Complaint: Rescue Ready indicator not showing green during routine check.
CCR.Cause: Battery and pads verified; self-test fails to complete successfully.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service and sent for repair; Out of Service label applied.

title: ZOLL AED Plus Defibrillator - Audio Prompt or Speaker Failure
description: Addresses missing, distorted, or absent audio prompts, focusing on speaker function, device settings, and external obstructions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active use. If a patient requires resuscitation, provide a backup AED. Never troubleshoot on a patient.

steps.title: Check Volume Settings
steps.instructions: Locate the AED volume control (if adjustable). Ensure it is not muted or set too low. Expected outcome: audible prompt when powered on.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the AED off, wait 10 seconds, then turn it back on. Observe if the audio prompt initiates. Sometimes software resets resolve minor glitches.

steps.title: Inspect Speaker Area
steps.instructions: Check for dust, debris, or obstruction over the speaker grille. Clean carefully if needed. Expected outcome: unobstructed sound output.

steps.title: Verify Battery Status
steps.instructions: Low or improperly seated batteries can reduce speaker performance. Confirm battery is recognized and fully charged. Expected outcome: normal audio prompts resume.

steps.title: Check Pads and Connections
steps.instructions: Some AEDs may suspend audio if pads are not connected properly. Ensure pads are installed and connectors fully seated. Expected outcome: device recognizes pads and enables prompts.

steps.title: Test with Built-In Self-Test or Demo Mode
steps.instructions: If available, activate the AED’s self-test or demo mode to confirm speaker function without patient involvement. Expected outcome: audio prompts audible.

CCR.Complaint: AED audio prompts not audible during device power-on.
CCR.Cause: Speaker malfunction or obstruction confirmed after external checks.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair.

title: ZOLL AED 3 Defibrillator - Battery Not Recognized or Smart Battery Communication Error
description: Addresses battery alerts, unrecognized or communication errors, focusing on battery seating, contacts, firmware, and external power issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If a patient requires resuscitation, provide a backup AED before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery
steps.instructions: Remove the battery and inspect for physical damage, corrosion, or dirt on contacts. Ensure the battery is within its expiration date.

steps.title: Reseat the Battery
steps.instructions: Reinsert the battery firmly until it clicks. Poor seating is a common cause of communication errors.

steps.title: Check Battery Orientation
steps.instructions: Verify the battery is installed in the correct orientation per device labeling. Incorrect insertion can prevent recognition.

steps.title: Clean Contacts
steps.instructions: Use a dry, lint-free cloth to gently clean both battery and device contacts. Dirt or residue can block communication.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the AED off, wait 10 seconds, and turn it back on. Observe if the battery is now recognized.

steps.title: Try a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: Insert a verified, fully charged battery to rule out battery failure. If the device accepts a new battery, the original battery may be defective.

steps.title: Inspect Firmware and Device Updates
steps.instructions: If available, confirm the AED firmware is current, as outdated software can occasionally cause battery communication errors.

CCR.Complaint: AED displayed “Battery Not Recognized” on power-on.
CCR.Cause: Original battery failed communication checks; contacts clean and device firmware up-to-date.
CCR.Resolution: Battery replaced with known good unit; AED confirmed operational. If original battery required service, noted for replacement.

title: ZOLL AED 3 Defibrillator - Pad Connection Errors or Pad Not Detected
description: Addresses pad connection errors or pads not detected, focusing on pad placement, cable integrity, and external connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If a patient requires resuscitation, provide a backup AED. Never troubleshoot on an active patient.

steps.title: Inspect Pads and Cables
steps.instructions: Check that the pads are the correct model for AED 3. Examine cables for cuts, kinks, or damage. Replace any visibly damaged pads or cables. Proper contact is required for device readiness.

steps.title: Confirm Pad Installation
steps.instructions: Remove and reinstall the pads. Ensure connectors click into place securely. Loose or improperly seated pads can prevent detection.

steps.title: Check Pad Expiration and Storage
steps.instructions: Verify that pads are within expiration date and have been stored per manufacturer guidelines. Expired or improperly stored pads may fail to connect.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn off the AED, wait 10 seconds, then power on. Observe if the device recognizes the pads. Sometimes a reset resolves detection issues caused by temporary errors.

steps.title: Inspect Device Pad Connector
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the device’s pad port for debris, corrosion, or bent pins. Clean gently with a dry, lint-free cloth. Ensure the port is free of obstruction.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Pads
steps.instructions: If available, connect a set of pads known to be functioning. If detected, the issue is likely with the original pads or cables.

CCR.Complaint: AED 3 displayed “Pad Not Detected” alert during daily check.
CCR.Cause: Original pads improperly seated and cables showed minor wear.
CCR.Resolution: Pads reseated and cables replaced; device successfully recognized pads on power-on.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested if battery is separate
Pads checked for expiration and storage history
Device successfully powered on after reseating pads

title: ZOLL AED 3 Defibrillator - Self-Test Failure or Status Indicator Fault
description: Addresses self-test failures or status indicator faults, focusing on battery, pad connections, device readiness, and external error conditions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If a patient requires resuscitation, provide a backup AED before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Battery and Power
steps.instructions:
Ensure the battery is correctly seated.
Inspect for corrosion or debris on battery contacts.
Replace with a known good battery if the device reports “battery error.”
Expected Outcome: AED powers on and self-test progresses.

steps.title: Verify Pad Installation
steps.instructions:
Ensure pads are connected firmly and correctly.
Inspect pad connectors and cables for damage or debris.
Replace pads if physical damage is noted.
Expected Outcome: Self-test recognizes pads.

steps.title: Inspect Status Indicators
steps.instructions:
Observe the device status lights and display messages during power-on.
Compare to AED 3 standard readiness indicators.
Expected Outcome: Indicators display normal status (green).

steps.title: Perform a Manual Self-Test (if available)
steps.instructions:
Follow AED prompts to initiate a manual self-test.
Record any error codes or abnormal indicator behavior.
Expected Outcome: Device completes self-test without faults.

steps.title: Check Firmware and Device Updates
steps.instructions:
Confirm device firmware is current.
Some self-test or indicator faults may be resolved with manufacturer-recommended updates.

CCR.Complaint: Device fails self-test and status indicator shows fault on power-on.
CCR.Cause: Battery seated correctly, pads verified; self-test failure persists. Likely internal fault.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled “Out of Service,” sent to manufacturer for evaluation.

title: ZOLL AED 3 Defibrillator - Touchscreen or Display Unresponsive
description: Addresses unresponsive touchscreen or display issues, focusing on external causes such as power, battery, screen contamination, and device readiness.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If a patient requires resuscitation, provide a backup AED. Troubleshooting must not delay patient care.

steps.title: Check Battery Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure the smart battery is correctly seated and locked. A poorly seated or unrecognized battery can prevent display initialization.

steps.title: Verify Battery Charge
steps.instructions: Confirm the battery has sufficient charge. Connect a fully charged backup battery if available and observe if the display responds.

steps.title: Inspect Screen Surface
steps.instructions: Clean the touchscreen with a soft, lint-free cloth. Remove any moisture, debris, or residue that may block capacitive touch input.

steps.title: Check for Screen Damage
steps.instructions: Look for cracks, scratches, or discoloration that could interfere with touch functionality. Note: internal damage cannot be repaired onsite.

steps.title: Power Cycle the AED
steps.instructions: Remove the battery, wait 10 seconds, then reinstall the battery and power on. Observe if the display initializes normally.

steps.title: Check External Connections
steps.instructions: Disconnect any attached accessories (pads, cables) and test the AED independently. Faulty accessories can sometimes prevent touchscreen operation.

steps.title: Observe Device Indicators
steps.instructions: Note any error messages, LED behavior, or audible alerts on power-up. These can help identify if a system fault exists beyond the touchscreen.

CCR.Complaint: AED 3 touchscreen unresponsive on power-on.
CCR.Cause: Display remains blank; battery seated correctly; no external debris detected.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service and sent for repair; labeled Out of Service.

title: ZOLL AED 3 Defibrillator - Wi-Fi / Connectivity or Cloud Monitoring Failure
description: Addresses connectivity issues preventing the AED from connecting to Wi-Fi, cloud monitoring, or reporting status, focusing on network, device settings, and external communication errors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If a patient requires resuscitation, provide a backup AED. Troubleshooting should never interfere with patient care.

steps.title: Verify Network Availability
steps.instructions: Check that the Wi-Fi network is active and functioning on other devices. An offline network will prevent cloud connectivity. Expected outcome: other devices can connect and access the internet.

steps.title: Confirm AED Wi-Fi Settings
steps.instructions: Access the AED settings and verify Wi-Fi is enabled, credentials are correct, and the network is within range. Incorrect SSID or password will block cloud communication.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Environment
steps.instructions: Ensure the AED is within range of the Wi-Fi signal, and there are no strong sources of interference (e.g., thick walls, microwaves, cordless phones). Expected outcome: improved signal strength.

steps.title: Restart AED and Network Hardware
steps.instructions: Power cycle the AED and, if possible, the Wi-Fi router. This can resolve temporary connection issues. Expected outcome: device reconnects to network.

steps.title: Check Firmware Version
steps.instructions: Ensure the AED is running the latest firmware. Outdated firmware can prevent successful cloud communication. If an update is available, apply it per manufacturer guidance.

steps.title: Observe Status Indicators
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED shows network connectivity (e.g., Wi-Fi icon) or successful cloud sync. If indicators remain offline, the issue may be internal or cloud-related.

CCR.Complaint: AED 3 fails to connect to Wi-Fi and cloud monitoring.
CCR.Cause: Network credentials verified; AED unable to establish connection despite active Wi-Fi.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for manufacturer evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Wi-Fi network tested with other devices
Final device status (Out of Service if unresolved)

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: CANNOT CHARGE
description: Troubleshooting guide for a ZOLL R Series CANNOT CHARGE error that prevents the defibrillator from charging for defibrillation or synchronized cardioversion.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If CANNOT CHARGE appears during patient care, remove the defibrillator from use, use another defibrillator if available, and do not continue attempting to charge the device on an active patient.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the ZOLL R Series, confirm whether CANNOT CHARGE appears when the CHARGE button is pressed, and note whether the failure occurs in manual defib mode, synchronized cardioversion mode, or both.

steps.title: Confirm the Device Is in the Correct Operating Mode
steps.instructions: Verify the selector switch is in the appropriate defibrillation mode, confirm the energy level is selected, and confirm the charge command is being given from the expected control location.

steps.title: Check AC Power and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to a known-good AC outlet, confirm AC power indicators, check battery status, install a known-good charged battery if available, and attempt to charge again in a safe test setup.

steps.title: Inspect Therapy Cables, Pads, and Paddles
steps.instructions: Inspect the therapy cable, pads, and paddles for damage, loose connections, bent pins, contamination, or wear. Replace external accessories with known-good compatible parts if available.

steps.title: Test With Known-Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Swap the therapy cable, pads, or paddles with known-good accessories and attempt a controlled charge test using approved test equipment or facility procedure.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the exterior for cracked housing, fluid intrusion, damaged connectors, corrosion, burnt smell, heat marks, or loose external parts. Do not open the unit during basic troubleshooting.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Functional Check
steps.instructions: Connect the defibrillator to a defibrillator analyzer or approved test load and attempt to charge to a selected energy level. Observe whether the unit charges or displays CANNOT CHARGE.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Failure Is External or Device-Related
steps.instructions: If the error clears with different power, battery, cable, pads, or paddles, document the failed external item. If the error remains, treat the issue as an internal high-voltage charging failure.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displays CANNOT CHARGE when the charge button is pressed. Unit is unable to charge for defibrillation.
CCR.Cause: Known-good AC power, battery, therapy cable, and pads/paddles checked. Error remained during controlled test setup, indicating likely internal high-voltage charging system failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defibrillator from service, labeled Out of Service, and escalated for repair/bench evaluation. High-voltage module or capacitor failure suspected.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: CHARGE FAILED
description: Troubleshooting guide for Error: CHARGE FAILED on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, including patient safety steps, external power and accessory checks, safe defibrillator testing, and escalation for likely charging or high-voltage subsystem failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient use immediately, transfer the patient to another working defibrillator or monitor/defibrillator if needed, and do not rely on a unit that cannot complete a charge attempt.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact displayed error is CHARGE FAILED and determine whether it occurs at startup, when CHARGE is pressed, during charge ramp-up, after partial charge, or during readiness testing.

steps.title: Check AC Power and Battery Status
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to a known-good hospital-grade outlet, verify AC indicators, check battery condition and seating, reseat the battery if appropriate, and try a known-good compatible battery if available.

steps.title: Check the Power Cord, Outlet, and Environment
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord, test the outlet, avoid questionable power strips, and rule out moisture, excessive heat, contamination, or other external environmental problems.

steps.title: Check Pads, Paddles, and Therapy Cable Setup
steps.instructions: Inspect the therapy cable, connector pins, pads, paddles, and selected therapy path. Try known-good accessories if available and remove any defective accessory from service.

steps.title: Perform a Safe Defibrillator Test
steps.instructions: Use an approved defibrillator analyzer or test load to confirm whether the unit fails immediately, partially charges then fails, charges but will not hold charge, or repeatedly displays CHARGE FAILED.

steps.title: Escalate if the Failure Remains
steps.instructions: If the error persists after external power, battery, accessory, setup, and test load checks, remove the unit from service and send it for repair or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series displays Error: CHARGE FAILED during defibrillator charge attempt. Unit will not complete charge cycle and is not ready for shock delivery.
CCR.Cause: Verified CHARGE FAILED during controlled test. AC power, battery seating, therapy cable, and test load were checked. Failure remained present, indicating likely internal defibrillator charging or high-voltage subsystem fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair or bench evaluation. Device was not returned to clinical use.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: TEST FAILED
description: Troubleshooting guide for TEST FAILED errors on the ZOLL R Series Defibrillator during readiness testing, PM checks, or 30J self-test failures that may indicate unsafe defibrillator performance.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient use if TEST FAILED is displayed. Replace it with a known-good defibrillator immediately and do not leave a failed unit staged for emergency use.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact displayed error is TEST FAILED. Determine whether it appears during startup, daily readiness testing, shift check, PM testing, or a 30J defibrillator test. Review the printed test strip or test record if available.

steps.title: Check AC Power and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to known-good AC power, confirm AC indicator behavior, verify the battery is installed correctly, and swap in a known-good charged battery if available. Power cycle the unit and repeat the test.

steps.title: Check Therapy Cable, Pads, Paddles, or Test Load
steps.instructions: Inspect the therapy cable, paddles, pads, connectors, and test load for damage, loose connections, contamination, or incorrect setup. Repeat the test using known-good external therapy accessories when available.

steps.title: Confirm the Correct Test Procedure
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit is in the correct mode, required accessories are connected, and the readiness or PM test is being performed according to facility procedure.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the housing, display, controls, therapy connector, ECG connector, printer door, and battery bay for impact damage, fluid residue, corrosion, overheating, loose parts, or damaged connectors.

steps.title: Run a Controlled Readiness Check
steps.instructions: Use known-good AC power, battery, therapy cable, paddles, pads, or test load. Run the self-test or 30J test again and review the test result or printed report.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Accessory-Related or Device-Related
steps.instructions: If the failure follows a battery, therapy cable, paddles, pads, or test accessory, remove that accessory from service. If the failure remains with known-good accessories and power, remove the defibrillator from service for bench evaluation or repair.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator reported TEST FAILED during readiness or PM testing and was removed from the clinical area.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed TEST FAILED during controlled testing. AC power, battery, therapy cable, paddles, pads, and test setup were checked. Failure persisted with known-good external accessories, indicating likely internal defibrillator readiness or high-voltage self-test fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defibrillator from service, labeled Out of Service, replaced with known-good unit, and sent for bench evaluation or vendor repair. Final device status: not returned to patient use.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: DEFIB FAULT 95
description: Troubleshooting guide for DEFIB FAULT 95 on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, indicating PD module communication failure that may affect defibrillation, pacing, charging, shock delivery, and readiness.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the ZOLL R Series from patient use, transfer care to a known-good defibrillator, and notify clinical staff that the affected unit is unavailable for emergency use.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the unit and confirm DEFIB FAULT 95 appears. Note whether it occurs at startup, during self-test, in defib mode, during charge attempt, or intermittently during operation.

steps.title: Remove the Unit From Clinical Use Before Further Checks
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, attach an Out of Service tag, and separate it from ready-use equipment so it cannot be used clinically.

steps.title: Check Basic Power Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to a known-good AC outlet, confirm AC power indication, verify the battery is seated, and test with a known-good charged battery if available.

steps.title: Check External Accessories and Patient Connections
steps.instructions: Disconnect pads, paddles, ECG leads, SpO₂ cables, NIBP hoses, therapy cables, and other accessories. Inspect connectors for damage, contamination, fluid intrusion, or loose fit.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit for Physical Damage or Fluid Intrusion
steps.instructions: Inspect the case, therapy connector, paddle wells, battery compartment, printer area, rear connectors, and display area for cracks, fluid residue, corrosion, heat, odor, or damage.

steps.title: Perform One Controlled Restart
steps.instructions: Power the unit off, remove AC power and battery, wait briefly, reinstall a known-good battery, reconnect AC power, and restart. Do not continue repeated resets if the fault returns.

steps.title: Review Readiness or Self-Test Results
steps.instructions: Check the readiness indicator or self-test result and record whether the failure affects defib, pacing, charge, shock, or general readiness.

steps.title: Isolate the Likely Failure Category
steps.instructions: Determine whether the issue is accessory-related, power-related, external damage-related, or internal/device-related based on whether the fault clears after external checks.

steps.title: Do Not Attempt Patient-Use Verification if the Fault Persists
steps.instructions: Keep the unit out of service and send it for repair or bench evaluation if DEFIB FAULT 95 remains active after external checks.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displayed DEFIB FAULT 95. Staff reported the unit failed readiness check and was not considered available for defibrillation use.
CCR.Cause: Verified DEFIB FAULT 95 during startup/self-test. Battery, AC power, external accessories, therapy cable connection, and visible damage checks did not resolve the fault. Fault indicates PD module communication failure, consistent with likely internal defibrillator subsystem failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and labeled Out of Service. Replaced clinical area coverage with known-good defibrillator. Sent unit for repair/bench evaluation for suspected PD engine/module communication failure. Final status: not returned to service.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: DEFIB FAULT 96
description: Troubleshooting guide for DEFIB FAULT 96 on a ZOLL R Series defibrillator, indicating a discharge-related PD module fault that may prevent reliable therapy delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ZOLL R Series from patient use immediately, transfer therapy and monitoring to a working defibrillator, do not repeatedly attempt shocks while the fault is active, and label the device unavailable until evaluated.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the screen shows DEFIB FAULT 96, determine whether it occurred during startup, self-test, charging, discharge, or after a failed shock, and document any additional readiness or test failure messages.

steps.title: Remove the Device From Clinical Service
steps.instructions: Apply an Out of Service tag, remove the device from the clinical area, notify leadership, and replace it with a ready spare defibrillator.

steps.title: Check Basic External Conditions First
steps.instructions: Confirm AC power, power cord condition, battery installation and charge status, and inspect the unit for damage, contamination, heat, smell, arcing marks, or unusual sounds.

steps.title: Inspect Therapy Cables, Pads, and Paddles
steps.instructions: Inspect the multifunction therapy cable, pads, paddles, connectors, discharge buttons, and cable insulation. Test with known-good, in-date accessories when safe and available.

steps.title: Perform a Safe Bench Test Using Approved Test Equipment
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to a calibrated defibrillator analyzer or approved test load, observe charge and discharge behavior, record delivered energy if discharge occurs, and document whether the error appears during or after discharge.

steps.title: Check Whether the Fault Is Accessory-Related or Device-Related
steps.instructions: Use known-good cables, pads, paddles, and test loads to determine whether the fault follows an accessory or remains with the defibrillator.

steps.title: Review Self-Test and Readiness Results
steps.instructions: Review readiness status, failed defibrillator self-tests, available printed logs, and whether the error occurred before or after self-test failure.

steps.title: Do Not Clear and Return Without Corrective Action
steps.instructions: Do not return the device to service just because the message disappears after a power cycle or one successful test. Escalate if the fault returns or cannot be confidently explained by an external issue.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If an external item caused the issue, replace it, complete full operational defibrillator testing, confirm the error does not return, document the repair, and return the device only after it passes required checks.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displayed DEFIB FAULT 96 during attempted discharge. Staff reported the unit charged but faulted when shock delivery was attempted.
CCR.Cause: Known-good power source, battery, therapy cable, pads, and test load were checked. Fault remained with the defibrillator. Error indicates PD module reported an error on discharge, consistent with likely internal PD engine failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and labeled Out of Service. Replaced with spare defibrillator for clinical area. Sent unit for repair or bench evaluation for PD engine replacement and post-repair defibrillator performance testing.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: DEFIB FAULT 79
description: Troubleshooting guide for DEFIB FAULT 79 on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, indicating a PD module defib/pace failure during POST that may prevent reliable therapy delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the defibrillator from patient use if DEFIB FAULT 79 is displayed. Replace it with a known-good defibrillator and do not use the affected unit for defibrillation, synchronized cardioversion, pacing, or patient monitoring if therapy functions are affected.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on, observe startup behavior, and confirm that DEFIB FAULT 79 appears during POST. Note whether the error is immediate, intermittent, or occurs after a readiness or self-test.

steps.title: Check AC Power and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the R Series to a known-good AC outlet, confirm AC power indication, check battery condition, and try a known-good compatible battery if available.

steps.title: Inspect Therapy Cables, Pads, and External Accessories
steps.instructions: Inspect defib and pacing cable connections, connector pins, strain relief, pads, and accessories. Test with known-good therapy accessories if available.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Physical Inspection
steps.instructions: Check the unit for cracks, impact damage, fluid intrusion, corrosion, contamination, damaged ports, abnormal sounds, burnt odor, or signs of overheating.

steps.title: Attempt a Controlled Restart
steps.instructions: Power the unit off, disconnect AC power briefly, remove and reseat the battery, reconnect AC power, and power the unit back on to observe POST.

steps.title: Run the Appropriate Readiness or Operational Check
steps.instructions: Follow the facility’s approved ZOLL R Series checkout process. Confirm the device completes readiness or self-test checks and that no defib, pacing, or charge-related faults return.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Accessory-Related or Device-Related
steps.instructions: If the fault clears after replacing external accessories and the unit passes testing, document the accessory issue. If DEFIB FAULT 79 persists with known-good accessories, treat it as an internal defib/pace subsystem failure.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displays DEFIB FAULT 79 during power-on POST. Unit fails startup readiness check and may not be reliable for defib or pacing use.
CCR.Cause: DEFIB FAULT 79 confirmed during POST. AC power, battery, external therapy cable, pads, and visible external condition checked. Fault persisted with basic external causes ruled out, indicating likely internal PD module defib/pace failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defibrillator from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for repair/bench evaluation due to persistent DEFIB FAULT 79. Service-level action may require PD engine replacement.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: DEFIB MAINT. REQ.
description: Troubleshooting guide for the ZOLL R Series DEFIB MAINT. REQ. message caused by high discharge count, indicating defibrillator maintenance is required before continued clinical reliance.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient use if DEFIB MAINT. REQ. is active. Place a known-ready backup defibrillator in service and notify clinical staff that the affected unit should not be used until evaluated.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the ZOLL R Series and confirm the exact displayed message is DEFIB MAINT. REQ. Note when it appears and document any additional service, test, charge, or defib fault messages.

steps.title: Understand the Maintenance Condition
steps.instructions: Recognize this as a service maintenance condition caused by more than 5000 discharges at 200J. Do not treat it as a normal user-reset alarm or accessory-related issue.

steps.title: Check for Other Active Faults
steps.instructions: Review the display for additional fault messages and perform readiness or bench testing only if allowed by facility procedure. Document any failed test results before escalation.

steps.title: Check External and Simple Causes First
steps.instructions: Confirm known-good AC power, battery condition, therapy cable or paddle condition, correct analyzer or test load connection, and inspect for physical damage, fluid intrusion, contamination, or overheating.

steps.title: Review Device History and Usage Context
steps.instructions: Check prior work orders, heavy-use locations, training or simulation use, and the date of the last defibrillator-related service or preventive maintenance.

steps.title: Do Not Clear and Return Without Service Evaluation
steps.instructions: Do not return the device to clinical service just because it powers on. Remove it from service, label it Out of Service, and escalate for service evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displayed DEFIB MAINT. REQ. during startup/readiness check. Unit removed from clinical area because maintenance-required message was active.
CCR.Cause: Device indicated required defibrillator maintenance due to accumulated high-energy discharge count. Basic external checks did not identify a power, battery, cable, accessory, or test setup issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service, labeled Out of Service, placed backup defibrillator in clinical area, and escalated device for service/repair evaluation of the defibrillator therapy subsystem.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: DEFIB DISABLED
description: Troubleshooting guide for ZOLL R Series DEFIB DISABLED errors where defibrillation therapy may be unavailable due to configuration, subsystem, or high-voltage failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the defibrillator from patient use, obtain a known-good replacement defibrillator, notify clinical staff, and do not attempt to use the device for patient defibrillation while DEFIB DISABLED is active.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on, confirm the exact DEFIB DISABLED message, check whether it appears alone or with companion faults, and document when the message appears.

steps.title: Remove the Unit From Clinical Service
steps.instructions: Place the device out of service, attach an Out of Service tag, move it to Clinical Engineering, and replace the clinical location with a known-good defibrillator.

steps.title: Check for Other Active Fault Messages
steps.instructions: Review the screen, event log, or service history for related messages such as DEFIB FAULT, CANNOT CHARGE, CHARGE FAILED, TEST FAILED, service prompts, or configuration-related messages.

steps.title: Check Basic Power and Battery Conditions
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to known-good AC power, confirm AC indication, install a known-good charged battery if available, inspect contacts, restart the unit, and observe whether the error remains.

steps.title: Inspect Therapy Cables, Pads, and External Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the therapy cable, pads, paddles, connectors, seating, expiration, contamination, and accessory compatibility. Try known-good accessories when available.

steps.title: Check Mode Selection and Configuration Concerns
steps.instructions: Confirm the operating mode, ask whether the issue started after configuration or service activity, and do not change clinical configuration settings unless authorized.

steps.title: Perform an Approved Operational Check
steps.instructions: Use a defibrillator analyzer or approved test load if allowed by department procedure. Confirm whether the unit can select defib mode, charge, hold charge, and discharge safely into the analyzer.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Accessory, Configuration, or Device Related
steps.instructions: Use the results of accessory, power, battery, configuration, and operational checks to decide whether the issue was external, configuration-related, or likely internal.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the error clears after correcting an external issue, complete required operational verification, confirm no faults return, document the corrected cause, and return the device to service only after all required checks pass.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displayed DEFIB DISABLED. Staff reported defibrillation function was unavailable and unit was removed from the clinical area.
CCR.Cause: DEFIB DISABLED condition confirmed. External power, battery, therapy cable, and pad connection checks did not clear the error. Persistent condition indicates likely internal defib subsystem, configuration, or high-voltage module fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, tagged Out of Service, replaced clinical area with known-good defibrillator, and escalated unit for bench evaluation/service repair.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: PACER DISABLED
description: Troubleshooting guide for PACER DISABLED on a ZOLL R Series defibrillator where pacing therapy is unavailable and the unit may require service.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient use if pacing is needed or PACER DISABLED is displayed during clinical use. Transfer the patient to another defibrillator/monitor capable of pacing.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact PACER DISABLED message and note whether it appears at startup, when selecting pacer mode, during self-test, after pad connection, or intermittently during use.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to known-good AC power, verify battery condition, install a known-good battery if available, and power cycle the unit after stable power is confirmed.

steps.title: Check Therapy Cable and Electrode Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the therapy cable, therapy connector, pads, and approved test load path. Swap with known-good accessories if available.

steps.title: Confirm This Is Not a Pad or Patient Connection Message
steps.instructions: Check for pads off, poor pad contact, check pads, or therapy cable messages. Replace pads with known-good compatible electrodes before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Device Configuration and Operating Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit is configured with pacing capability and that pacing mode is not locked out by configuration, service state, or restricted mode.

steps.title: Run the Appropriate Readiness or Operational Check
steps.instructions: Perform the facility-approved operational check and record whether pacing verification passes or fails, including any additional faults or maintenance messages.

steps.title: Isolate Accessory vs Device Failure
steps.instructions: Use known-good power, battery, therapy cable, pads, and test load to determine whether the fault follows an accessory or remains with the defibrillator.

steps.title: Do Not Return to Service With PACER DISABLED Active
steps.instructions: If PACER DISABLED remains after external checks, remove the unit from service, label it Out of Service, notify staff, and send it for repair or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series displays PACER DISABLED. Staff reported pacer function unavailable when attempting to use or verify pacing mode.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed PACER DISABLED message. Power source, battery, therapy cable, pads, and basic external connection issues were checked. Error remained, indicating likely internal pacing-related failure or service-required condition.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defibrillator from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench evaluation/vendor repair. Staff notified to use alternate defibrillator with verified pacing capability.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: PACER FAULT 115
description: Troubleshooting guide for PACER FAULT 115 on a ZOLL R Series defibrillator, indicating a pacer failure during POST that may disable pacing and require service evaluation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ZOLL R Series from patient use if pacing may be needed. Use another defibrillator or pacing-capable device and notify staff that pacing function may not be available.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the device on, confirm PACER FAULT 115 appears during startup or POST, document whether it is repeatable or intermittent, and check whether pacing is disabled or unavailable.

steps.title: Check Battery and AC Power
steps.instructions: Use a known-good AC power source and charged ZOLL battery, then power cycle the device and observe whether the pacer fault returns.

steps.title: Inspect External Pacing Accessories
steps.instructions: Inspect the therapy cable, connectors, pads, and test load. Swap with known-good accessories when available to rule out external cable or connection issues.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the housing, controls, therapy connector area, and external surfaces for impact damage, fluid intrusion, corrosion, contamination, or cracked parts.

steps.title: Attempt a Controlled Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the device off, disconnect AC power, remove the battery, wait briefly, reinstall a known-good battery, reconnect AC power, and observe startup behavior.

steps.title: Run the Appropriate Readiness or Operational Check
steps.instructions: Run the facility-approved ZOLL R Series operational check and verify that required readiness, defibrillation, and pacing-related checks pass before returning the device to service.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Pattern
steps.instructions: Determine whether the fault follows the power source, battery, therapy cable, pads, test load, damaged connector, or the device itself.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the fault clears after correcting an external issue and the device passes all required checks, return it to service and document the correction.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series reported PACER FAULT 115 during startup/POST. Pacing function may be disabled or unavailable. Unit removed from clinical use for evaluation.
CCR.Cause: PACER FAULT 115 reproduced during startup. Battery, AC power source, therapy cable, pads/test load, and external connections checked. Fault persisted, indicating likely internal pacer/therapy subsystem failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation/repair. Clinical area notified to use alternate pacing-capable defibrillator.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: PACER FAULT 117
description: Troubleshooting guide for PACER FAULT 117 on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, indicating a pacer functional safety failure while running that may compromise transcutaneous pacing.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from patient use if PACER FAULT 117 is active. Transfer patient care to a known-good defibrillator/pacer if pacing, defibrillation, or monitoring is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe area and confirm that PACER FAULT 117 is displayed. Document whether the error appears during startup, self-test, pacer mode selection, or while operating.

steps.title: Remove the Device From Clinical Service
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from the care area, apply an Out of Service tag, notify staff, and place a known-good replacement device in service.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Install a known-good charged battery, connect AC power, verify AC indication, inspect battery contacts, and power cycle after confirming stable power.

steps.title: Check Therapy Cable and Pacing Accessories
steps.instructions: Inspect the multifunction therapy cable, connectors, pins, strain reliefs, pads, and test load. Test with known-good accessories when available.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Physical or Fluid Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the housing, therapy connector, battery well, AC power connection, and controls for impact damage, contamination, heat, odor, or visible defects.

steps.title: Run an Operational Check in a Bench Environment
steps.instructions: Use a defibrillator analyzer capable of pacer testing to verify whether the fault returns and whether pacing output can be selected and detected.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Accessory-Related or Device-Related
steps.instructions: If the fault clears after replacing external accessories, repeat the full operational check. If it remains with known-good power, cable, and test load, treat it as an internal pacing or therapy subsystem failure.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator reported PACER FAULT 117. Staff reported a pacer fault while the unit was running, creating concern that transcutaneous pacing may be unavailable or unsafe.
CCR.Cause: PACER FAULT 117 was confirmed during bench evaluation. Battery, AC power, therapy cable, and external pacing accessories were checked. Fault remained with known-good external setup, indicating likely internal pacer or therapy subsystem failure.
CCR.Resolution: Device was removed from service and labeled Out of Service. Replacement defibrillator was placed in the clinical area. Unit was sent for repair or qualified bench evaluation due to persistent pacer functional safety fault.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: ECG DISABLED
description: Troubleshooting guide for ECG DISABLED on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, including ECG cable checks, simulator testing, pads monitoring checks, power verification, and escalation for suspected ECG hardware failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient use if ECG monitoring is unavailable, unreliable, frozen, or disabled. Transfer the patient to another monitor or defibrillator if ECG monitoring is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the ZOLL R Series and confirm the displayed message reads exactly ECG DISABLED. Note when the error appears and whether ECG is affected through leads, pads, or both.

steps.title: Check ECG Cable and Lead Set
steps.instructions: Inspect the ECG cable connector and lead wires for bent pins, damage, contamination, corrosion, looseness, or strain damage. Install a known-good ECG cable and lead set.

steps.title: Check ECG Electrodes and Simulator Connection
steps.instructions: Use an ECG simulator or approved ECG test source. Confirm the simulator is producing a valid ECG signal and check whether the R Series displays a waveform or heart rate.

steps.title: Check Pads Monitoring Separately
steps.instructions: Connect a known-good ZOLL therapy cable and compatible test load or analyzer. Select pads monitoring if available and verify whether ECG displays through pads.

steps.title: Confirm the Error Is Not a Setup Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm the selected ECG source matches the connected accessory, the ECG cable is fully seated, and the selected lead is valid. Separate ECG DISABLED from normal lead-off or poor-contact messages.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Defibrillator
steps.instructions: After patient care has been transferred, turn the R Series off, disconnect patient accessories, reconnect known-good power, restart the unit, and observe whether ECG DISABLED returns.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Connect to a known-good AC outlet, confirm the AC indicator, install a known-good charged battery, and watch for power instability, unexpected shutdowns, or multiple unrelated errors.

steps.title: Inspect the Exterior for Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the ECG connector area, housing, controls, and ports for fluid intrusion, impact damage, residue, burnt odor, or other evidence of damage.

steps.title: Run Available Self-Test or Readiness Checks
