steps.instructions: Run standard operator-accessible readiness or self-test checks. Review printed or displayed results for ECG-related failures or repeated critical hardware messages.

CCR.Complaint: User reported ZOLL R Series displaying ECG DISABLED. ECG monitoring unavailable during startup/use. Device removed from clinical area for evaluation.
CCR.Cause: Verified ECG DISABLED message. Known-good ECG cable, lead set, simulator connection, pads monitoring check, power source, and accessory checks did not clear the error. Condition consistent with persistent ECG hardware failure involving the ECG module or analog board path.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defibrillator from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for repair/bench evaluation for suspected analog board or ECG hardware failure. Device not returned to patient use.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: ECG FAULT 7
description: Troubleshooting guide for ECG FAULT 7 on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, indicating the ECG processor is not responding and ECG monitoring may be unavailable or unreliable.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ZOLL R Series from patient use if ECG monitoring is unavailable, unreliable, frozen, or showing ECG FAULT 7. Use another defibrillator, bedside monitor, or transport monitor for active patient monitoring.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the displayed message is ECG FAULT 7 and document whether it appears at startup, during self-test, when ECG leads are connected, during monitoring, or intermittently during use.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Startup Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to known-good AC power, confirm battery status, install a known-good charged battery if needed, and restart the unit to see whether the fault returns.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Restart
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, disconnect ECG accessories, wait briefly, then power the unit back on using AC power and a known-good battery. Watch for ECG FAULT 7 during startup.

steps.title: Inspect the ECG Cable and Connector
steps.instructions: Inspect the ECG cable and ECG input connector for bent pins, cracked connectors, broken strain relief, fluid contamination, loose fit, damaged wires, debris, corrosion, or visible damage.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good ECG Cable
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible ZOLL R Series ECG cable and test with an ECG simulator if available. If the waveform returns and the fault clears, replace the defective ECG cable.

steps.title: Check ECG Lead Configuration and Accessory Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the selected ECG source matches the connected accessory and verify that leads, pads, paddles, electrodes, snaps, and lead wires are configured correctly.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the unit for cracked housing, evidence of a drop, fluid intrusion, corrosion, damage around the ECG input area, loose connectors, burning smell, or abnormal heat.

steps.title: Confirm Whether the Fault Follows the Accessory or Stays with the Device
steps.instructions: Test the suspect ECG cable on another compatible unit if available and test the affected defibrillator with a known-good cable and ECG simulator. If the fault stays with the defibrillator, suspect internal ECG hardware failure.

steps.title: Determine if the Error Is Persistent
steps.instructions: After external checks, power cycle the unit again. If ECG FAULT 7 persists, remove the device from service and send it for bench evaluation, approved repair, or vendor service. The manual-indicated technical action is replacement of the analog board.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator reported ECG FAULT 7. Staff stated ECG monitoring was unavailable and no reliable ECG waveform was displayed during use.
CCR.Cause: Verified ECG FAULT 7 on startup. Known-good AC power, charged battery, restart, ECG cable replacement, ECG simulator, and lead setup checks did not clear the fault. Persistent error indicates ECG processor not responding, likely internal analog board failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench repair or vendor evaluation for suspected analog board failure. Unit not returned to service pending repair and functional verification.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: ECG FAULT 200
description: Troubleshooting guide for ECG FAULT 200 on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, where the ECG application is unavailable and ECG monitoring or normal readiness may be affected.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ZOLL R Series from patient use if ECG monitoring, pacing, cardioversion, or defibrillation readiness is affected. Transfer the patient to another monitor/defibrillator if active monitoring or therapy is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on and confirm the exact displayed message is ECG FAULT 200. Note whether the error appears at startup, in ECG monitoring, during lead selection, after self-test, or intermittently during use.

steps.title: Check ECG Cable and Lead Setup First
steps.instructions: Inspect the ECG trunk cable and lead wires for bent pins, cracked connectors, contamination, fluid intrusion, or damaged insulation. Reconnect securely and test with a known-good ECG cable and lead set if available.

steps.title: Check Electrodes and Simulator Input
steps.instructions: Use a known-good ECG simulator or fresh electrodes if available. Confirm whether an ECG waveform appears and whether lead selection is possible.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: Power the unit off, disconnect external power, remove the battery if appropriate, wait briefly, then reinstall the battery, reconnect AC power, and restart the unit. Observe whether the ECG application becomes available.

steps.title: Check Battery and AC Power Stability
steps.instructions: Connect the device to a known-good AC outlet, verify AC power indication, install a known-good battery if available, and restart the unit using stable power.

steps.title: Check for Recent Software, Service, or Configuration Changes
steps.instructions: Determine whether the fault appeared after software loading, configuration changes, main board replacement, MCU-related service, failed software update, unexpected shutdown during update, or battery depletion during service.

steps.title: Do Not Treat This Like a Simple ECG Lead Failure
steps.instructions: After basic external checks are complete, do not continue swapping electrodes, pads, or cables if the error remains. Treat ECG FAULT 200 as an ECG application availability issue.

steps.title: Escalate for Software Reload
steps.instructions: If ECG FAULT 200 remains after restart, power checks, and basic ECG accessory checks, remove the device from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for bench evaluation or approved software reload.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displayed ECG FAULT 200 at startup. ECG monitoring function was unavailable and device could not be confirmed ready for patient use.
CCR.Cause: External ECG cable, lead set, power source, and restart checks did not clear the fault. Error indicated no ECG application available, consistent with software or application loading issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench evaluation and approved reload of MCU software and ECG application software. Device not returned to service pending repair and operational verification.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: CHECK ECG CABLE
description: Troubleshooting guide for CHECK ECG CABLE errors on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator, including ECG cable connection checks, source selection, leadwire inspection, known-good cable testing, ECG simulator verification, and service escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from patient use if ECG monitoring is unreliable. Transfer the patient to another monitor or defibrillator if continuous ECG monitoring or therapy capability is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display reads CHECK ECG CABLE and document whether it appears at startup, during ECG monitoring, when the cable is moved, or intermittently during use.

steps.title: Confirm the Correct ECG Source Is Selected
steps.instructions: Verify the selected ECG source matches the connected accessory, such as ECG cable, pads, or paddles.

steps.title: Inspect the ECG Cable Connection at the Device
steps.instructions: Disconnect and inspect the ECG connector for bent pins, loose fit, cracked housing, contamination, fluid residue, damaged locking tabs, or worn connector hardware.

steps.title: Inspect the ECG Cable and Leadwires
steps.instructions: Check the ECG trunk cable and leadwires for cuts, kinks, pinched sections, exposed conductors, broken strain relief, loose connections, corrosion, or residue.

steps.title: Replace the ECG Cable With a Known-Good Cable
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible ZOLL R Series ECG cable and check whether the CHECK ECG CABLE message clears.

steps.title: Check the Electrodes and Patient Lead Connections
steps.instructions: Use fresh electrodes, confirm proper adhesion, check leadwire placement, and verify secure snap or clip connections.

steps.title: Check for Contamination or Cleaning Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the ECG port and cable connector for moisture, cleaning fluid residue, corrosion, sticky residue, or visible fluid intrusion.

steps.title: Test ECG Monitoring Function
steps.instructions: Connect the device to an ECG simulator using a known-good ECG cable and confirm stable waveform, heart rate display, proper lead selection, and no CHECK ECG CABLE message.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Cable-Related or Device-Related
steps.instructions: If the error clears with a known-good cable, replace the accessory. If multiple known-good cables fail, remove the defibrillator from service for bench evaluation or repair.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator reported CHECK ECG CABLE error. Staff stated ECG waveform was not displaying reliably through the ECG cable.
CCR.Cause: ECG trunk cable failed cable recognition testing. CHECK ECG CABLE error cleared when a known-good compatible ECG cable was installed.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced defective ECG cable. Verified ECG monitoring using ECG simulator. Confirmed stable waveform, heart rate display, and no CHECK ECG CABLE error. Device returned to service.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: CHECK RECORDER
description: Troubleshooting guide for ZOLL R Series CHECK RECORDER errors caused by recorder paper problems, paper jams, door or latch issues, loading errors, or recorder hardware faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot the recorder while the defibrillator is needed for active patient care. Remove the unit from patient use if documentation, event printing, or readiness confidence is affected, and use another defibrillator if patient care is active or expected.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm that the exact displayed message is CHECK RECORDER. Observe whether the error appears at startup, during printing, after pressing the recorder button, or intermittently. Note whether the recorder fails to print, feeds poorly, makes noise without moving paper, stops mid-print, or shows no response.

steps.title: Check the Recorder Paper Supply
steps.instructions: Open the recorder compartment and verify that paper is installed. Check for an empty, loose, crushed, wet, contaminated, or backward paper roll. Install a known-good ZOLL-compatible recorder paper roll if needed and attempt a test print.

steps.title: Check for Paper Jam or Obstruction
steps.instructions: Inspect the recorder paper path for torn paper, folded paper, adhesive residue, labels, debris, or paper fragments. Clear any obstruction, confirm smooth paper feed, close the door, and run a test print.

steps.title: Confirm the Recorder Door Is Fully Closed
steps.instructions: Open and close the recorder door firmly. Confirm the latch engages and inspect for cracks, broken hinges, loose latch points, or anything preventing full closure. Perform a test strip if reseating the door clears the error.

steps.title: Verify Correct Paper Orientation
steps.instructions: Confirm that the thermal paper is loaded with the print side facing the correct direction for the recorder head. Feed the paper through the recorder path properly and run a test print.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the recorder door, latch, paper roller, and visible paper path for damage, fluid contamination, debris, burnt smell, grinding, clicking, or other abnormal signs. Remove the unit from service if damage or contamination is found.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck
steps.instructions: After external checks are complete, turn the unit off, wait briefly, and power it back on. Confirm whether CHECK RECORDER returns. If the message clears, complete a successful test print before returning the device to service.

steps.title: Isolate User-Correctable vs Device-Related Failure
steps.instructions: If paper supply, paper loading, paper path, recorder door, latch condition, and power cycle checks do not resolve the problem, treat the condition as a likely recorder assembly, sensor, latch detection, motor, thermal print mechanism, or internal control issue.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series displayed CHECK RECORDER error and staff reported the recorder would not print a rhythm strip during use.
CCR.Cause: Recorder paper path inspected. Paper roll was empty and recorder could not feed paper. No physical damage was found.
CCR.Resolution: Installed new compatible recorder paper roll, verified correct orientation, closed recorder door, power cycled unit, and completed successful test print. Device returned to service.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: RECORDER FAULT 142
description: Troubleshooting guide for ZOLL R Series RECORDER FAULT 142, a strip chart recorder system error that may affect printing, paper feed, event strips, code summary output, or recorder communication.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ZOLL R Series from patient use if the recorder fault affects documentation printing, event strip output, or confidence in device reliability. Use another defibrillator if monitoring, pacing, cardioversion, or defibrillation may be needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact displayed message is RECORDER FAULT 142. Determine whether it appears at startup, during self-test, when pressing the recorder button, during rhythm strip printing, during code summary printing, or intermittently during use.

steps.title: Check Recorder Paper First
steps.instructions: Confirm paper is installed, loaded in the correct direction, and not jammed, torn, folded, wet, or obstructed. Reload the paper and close the recorder door firmly before retesting.

steps.title: Inspect the Recorder Door and Latch
steps.instructions: Open and close the recorder door. Check for cracked plastic, loose hinges, broken latch tabs, debris, or anything preventing the door from sitting flush with the recorder assembly.

steps.title: Check for Paper Path Obstruction
steps.instructions: Remove the paper roll and inspect the strip chart path for paper fragments, adhesive residue, dirt, or foreign material. Do not force tools into the recorder mechanism. Reload paper and retest printing.

steps.title: Check Power and Startup Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit off, connect to known good AC power, confirm normal AC indicator behavior, power the unit on, allow startup to complete, and retest the recorder.

steps.title: Test Recorder Function
steps.instructions: With the device out of patient use, press the recorder or print button. Note whether the recorder starts printing, feeds paper, prints blank paper, prints distorted output, makes abnormal motor noise, or fails immediately with RECORDER FAULT 142.

steps.title: Inspect External Physical Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the recorder area and housing for cracks, fluid residue, missing parts, bent components, evidence of drops, burning smell, heat damage, or contamination. Remove the device from service if damage or fluid intrusion is found.

steps.title: Narrow the Fault Area
steps.instructions: Rule out paper loading, paper jam, recorder door, paper path obstruction, and power/startup issues before suspecting the recorder assembly, motor, printhead, sensor, internal interconnect, or digital board control path.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the recorder prints normally and RECORDER FAULT 142 no longer appears, reload paper properly, run an operational check, confirm defibrillator, monitor, and recorder functions pass, document the correction, and return the device to service.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series displayed RECORDER FAULT 142 and staff reported the strip chart recorder would not print during recorder test.
CCR.Cause: Recorder paper was loaded correctly and no paper jam, door issue, power issue, or external obstruction was found. Fault remained present during recorder test, indicating likely internal recorder assembly, interconnect, or digital board path failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation/repair. Recorder fault remained present after external checks.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: BATTERY COMMS ERROR
description: Troubleshooting guide for BATTERY COMMS ERROR on the ZOLL R Series defibrillator when the device cannot properly communicate with the installed battery, affecting battery recognition, status reporting, and portable-use reliability.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient use if BATTERY COMMS ERROR is active. Replace it with a known-good defibrillator if emergency defibrillation, pacing, cardioversion, or monitoring may be needed. Do not rely on battery operation while this error is present.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the ZOLL R Series on and confirm the displayed message is BATTERY COMMS ERROR. Note whether it appears at startup, after battery insertion, while running on battery power, or intermittently. Observe whether the unit recognizes the battery and whether it operates normally on AC power.

steps.title: Check AC Power First
steps.instructions: Connect the unit to a known-good AC power source. Confirm the AC indicator is present, allow the unit to remain connected briefly, and power cycle the device while on AC power.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery
steps.instructions: Remove the installed battery and inspect it for cracked housing, swelling, contamination, leakage, damaged contacts, or impact damage. Confirm the battery is compatible with the ZOLL R Series. Do not use a physically damaged battery.

steps.title: Reseat the Battery
steps.instructions: Remove the battery completely, inspect the battery compartment for debris or obstruction, reinstall the battery fully, confirm it latches securely, and power the device back on.

steps.title: Try a Known-Good Battery
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible ZOLL R Series battery and observe whether BATTERY COMMS ERROR returns. If the error clears, the original battery is likely defective. If the error remains, the issue likely stays with the defibrillator.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Battery in Another Compatible Unit
steps.instructions: If available, install the suspect battery into another known-good compatible R Series unit. If the same error appears, remove the battery from service. If the second unit reads the battery normally, suspect the original defibrillator.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery Compartment and Contacts
steps.instructions: Inspect the battery bay for bent contacts, recessed pins, contamination, corrosion, loose parts, liquid intrusion, or uneven battery seating. Do not scrape, bend, force, or modify internal contacts.

steps.title: Confirm Charging and Battery Recognition Behavior
steps.instructions: With AC connected and a known-good battery installed, check for expected charging and battery status behavior. Run the facility-approved operational checkout if the error clears.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Battery-Related or Device-Related
steps.instructions: Use battery swap results to determine whether the error follows one battery or remains with the defibrillator. Remove the unit from service if the error remains with multiple known-good batteries or if the battery bay appears damaged.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator displayed BATTERY COMMS ERROR and staff reported the unit would not properly recognize or communicate with the installed battery.
CCR.Cause: Battery communication fault confirmed. AC power was verified, the battery was reseated, the battery bay was inspected, and a known-good compatible battery was tested. Error remained with the defibrillator, indicating likely device-side battery communication failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defibrillator from service and labeled Out of Service. Documented battery IDs tested and sent unit for bench evaluation or repair for suspected battery interconnect or device-side communication issue.

title: ZOLL R Series Defibrillator - Error: BATT HIGH VOLTAGE
description: Troubleshooting guide for a ZOLL R Series BATT HIGH VOLTAGE error indicating charger voltage is too high, which may affect battery charging, AC operation, and defibrillator readiness.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient use if the BATT HIGH VOLTAGE error is active or recurring. Transfer defibrillation, pacing, and monitoring needs to another ready defibrillator. Do not rely on a defibrillator with an unresolved battery or charging system error.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm that the displayed message reads BATT HIGH VOLTAGE. Determine whether the error appears on startup, while connected to AC power, while running on battery, during charging, or intermittently after being stored on charge.

steps.title: Remove the Device From AC Power and Check Battery Operation
steps.instructions: Unplug the R Series from AC power and power the unit on using battery power only. Observe whether the error remains, clears, or changes to help separate AC charger behavior from battery-related behavior.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery Pack
steps.instructions: Remove the battery and inspect it for cracked housing, swelling, leaking, burn marks, damaged contacts, contamination, or loose fit. Remove any damaged, hot, swollen, or leaking battery from service.

steps.title: Reseat the Battery
steps.instructions: Power the defibrillator off, remove the battery, inspect the contacts and battery compartment, reinstall the battery firmly, and power the unit back on. Confirm whether the error returns.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Battery
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible ZOLL R Series battery. Power the unit on, connect AC power, and observe charging behavior. If the error clears, the original battery is suspect. If it remains, the device charger or AC charging circuit is suspect.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source and Power Cord
steps.instructions: Verify the device is connected to a proper hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the AC cord for damage, loose plug, bent prongs, damaged strain relief, or intermittent connection. Try a known-good cord and verified outlet if available.

steps.title: Observe Charging Behavior on AC Power
steps.instructions: With a known-good battery installed, connect AC power and watch for immediate return of the error, abnormal battery indication, failure to charge, service indicators, heat, odor, or unusual sounds.

steps.title: Run Required Readiness or Operational Checks
steps.instructions: If the error clears, perform the facility-required defibrillator readiness check. Verify normal power-on behavior, no active error message, normal battery and AC indicators, passing readiness status, and required ECG, printer, and alarm functions.

CCR.Complaint: ZOLL R Series defibrillator reported with BATT HIGH VOLTAGE error. Staff stated unit displayed a battery/charger fault while connected to AC power and may not be maintaining normal battery charge readiness.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed BATT HIGH VOLTAGE error. Battery and charging system issue observed. External checks performed including battery reseat, battery inspection, AC cord and outlet check, and known-good battery test. Error followed original battery or remained with known-good battery.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from patient use during troubleshooting. Tested with known-good battery and verified AC power source. Replaced failed battery and completed readiness check, or labeled unit Out of Service and sent for bench evaluation for suspected AC charger fault.
